17932ddb855ec30d10266e0d9fda5bc0c3075f79 fix(polymarket): fix CLOB auth — derive fallback + correct HMAC encoding
Three bugs fixed:
- POST /auth/api-key returns 400 if keys already exist; fall back to
  GET /auth/derive-api-key to retrieve existing credentials
- Map apiKey field correctly: creds.apiKey || creds.key
- HMAC digest was using Node base64url (strips = padding) instead of
  the TS clob-client approach: base64 + manual +→- /→_ substitution
  (preserves = padding), which is what Polymarket's server validates

- Add two new troubleshooting rows: insufficient gas, skip-fund retry
- Add "Why EOA Needs POL" section explaining x402-pay vs Polymarket difference

polymarket-skill.md (new):
- Compact, context-window-efficient reference — no repetition
- Covers setup, all commands, buy flow, neg risk, flags, errors, contracts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: 06f6fb0c238b

43b3547f7d181c9be724853cfc037194f9bcaff8 fix(polymarket): neg risk split, EOA gas, skip-fund flag + implementation docs
On-chain fixes (confirmed working live 2026-02-24):
- splitPosition for neg-risk markets now calls NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition(bytes32,uint256)
  instead of CTF.splitPosition — neg risk conditions must go through the adapter
- USDC.e approval target for neg-risk markets is NegRiskAdapter (not CTF Exchange);
  the adapter wraps USDC.e to wcol before calling CTF internally
- Pass negRisk flag from Gamma market data through to splitPosition()

UX:
- Add --skip-fund flag to polymarket buy: skips smart wallet→EOA transfer and uses
  EOA's existing USDC.e balance — avoids double-spend when retrying after partial failure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: 06f6fb0c238b

2b157e3cf803d61b0d170d517f891a710b579708 docs(polymarket): add POLYMARKET.md with full command reference and bet flow
Covers all 6 CLI commands with options, output fields, and examples.
Includes step-by-step flow from setup → market discovery → dry-run →
broadcast → check position → cancel, plus troubleshooting table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

a67a9cb7f1e09fa2fe1898ead3d46202d6c300bd fix(polymarket): correct CLOB auth, search, and orders endpoint
- L1 auth: fix EIP-712 field types (address not string), header names
  use underscores (POLY_ADDRESS) per py-clob-client spec
- L2 HMAC: base64-URL decode secret before signing; base64-URL encode
  output; use underscore header names
- GET orders: fix endpoint /orders → /data/orders
- getMarkets: client-side search filter (Gamma has no server-side FTS)
- getMarket: scan top markets to find by conditionId (Gamma conditionId
  param is ignored server-side)

Note: POST /auth/api-key returns 400 for EOAs with no Polymarket
account history — address must have interacted with Polymarket first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: 9f869c12047d

acdcb582e91f9f1cd9e2eb2b9043ef98a4445e49 feat(polymarket): add Polymarket prediction market operations
Adds 6 new CLI commands under `polygon-agent polymarket`:
- markets  — list active markets from Gamma API
- market   — get single market by conditionId
- buy      — split USDC.e → YES/NO tokens, sell unwanted side on CLOB
- positions — open positions for smart wallet (Data API)
- orders   — open CLOB orders for builder EOA
- cancel   — cancel a CLOB order by ID

Architecture follows x402-pay pattern: smart wallet funds builder EOA,
EOA handles all on-chain (approve, splitPosition) and CLOB signing
(HMAC-SHA256 auth, EIP-712 order structs via viem signTypedData).

New files:
- lib/polymarket.mjs — Gamma/CLOB/Data API clients + viem on-chain ops
- cli/commands/polymarket.mjs — 6 command functions

No new npm dependencies (viem already installed, crypto is Node built-in).

Higher TVL means tighter spreads, deeper liquidity, and lower
manipulation risk — a safer default for agent deposits.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

72ba456f1b9dca7e70c46eb04821c3a9c42982fa feat: deposit command using Trails earn pool discovery
- Add 'polygon-agent deposit --asset USDC --amount 0.3' command
- Uses TrailsApi.getEarnPools to discover the highest-APY active pool
  for the asset on the current chain (no hardcoded protocol addresses)
- Supports aave (supply) and morpho (ERC-4626 deposit) protocol encoding
- Dry-run shows pool name, APY, TVL, deposit address before committing
- Friendly error message if session lacks contract permission, with exact
  'wallet create --contract <addr>' command to fix it
- Update SKILL.md: document deposit, cross-chain swap (--to-chain),
  and session contract whitelisting for DeFi protocols

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ab1d1c965474f57a1cef6230fdaf736be1d9a0b1 fix: prefer USDC fee when wallet holds both USDC and POL
- Fix ReferenceError: session.projectAccessKey was not in scope inside
  runDappClientTx; replaced with projectAccessKey from destructuring
  (indexer cross-reference was silently failing for every tx)
- After indexer balance check, prefer native USDC address, then any
  USDC symbol, then first held fee token — so wallets with both POL
  and USDC always pay fees in USDC

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

0d6ac49ab5b8a065081e0182d1625793c3beaae1 Merge pull request #17 from 0xPolygon/fix/fee-selection-prefer-usdc
fix: prefer ERC20 (USDC) fee over native POL when wallet has no POL
2877a9484d11f2d1d8390d3bcba7ce29ef57a9ae fix: prefer ERC20 (USDC) fee over native POL when wallet has no POL
- Fix broken native fee detection: zero address "0x000...000" is a
  truthy string, so !o?.token?.contractAddress was always false for
  native POL option; switched to explicit zero-address check
- Remove dangerous fallback to feeOptions[0] (often POL) when native
  not found — this caused SimulationFailed when wallet had no POL
- Change sendToken to preferNativeFee: false so ERC20 sends always
  use the ERC20 fee path (getFeeTokens → USDC), not simulation-based
  getFeeOptions which can return POL as first option

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

657abc3f58261d09fa5bef975ddba80737d033d9 Merge pull request #16 from 0xPolygon/fix/ngrok-no-authtoken-required
fix: ngrok tunnel works without NGROK_AUTHTOKEN env var
e44a978386cbdcba963ea83ab5da1fc96d675856 fix: ngrok tunnel works without NGROK_AUTHTOKEN env var
authtoken_from_env: true forced the SDK to only look at the env var,
causing a silent fallback to localhost when it wasn't set. Now the SDK
resolves auth from the local ngrok config (~/.ngrok2/ngrok.yml) by
default, with NGROK_AUTHTOKEN as an explicit override — matching how
the system ngrok binary behaves.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

dd0b6ac44e4077c8246c8d0bf9dc5d906dec6893 Merge pull request #15 from 0xPolygon/fix/wrangler-assets-binding-and-url-ux
fix: add main entry point to wrangler.toml for SPA fallback worker
24a3a8a4faeffeb53bc8f25af9c98b106a6aa42f fix: add main entry point to wrangler.toml for SPA fallback worker
wrangler 4.x rejects binding = "ASSETS" on assets-only Workers.
Adding main = "worker.mjs" tells wrangler there is a Worker script,
enabling env.ASSETS and the /link SPA fallback route.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

a515aeaf735c1debdbf470c542923c27b9d87d32 Merge pull request #14 from 0xPolygon/fix/wrangler-assets-binding-and-url-ux
Fix/wrangler assets binding and url ux
4bc081b76890b10bd5bf00a08855911163a6d5aa feat: print clickable wallet session URL to stderr after JSON output
After the JSON blob, print the approval URL on its own line so
terminals (iTerm2, VS Code, etc.) auto-hyperlink it for easy clicking.

resolves auth from the local ngrok config (~/.ngrok2/ngrok.yml) by
default, with NGROK_AUTHTOKEN as an explicit override — matching how
the system ngrok binary behaves.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

24a3a8a4faeffeb53bc8f25af9c98b106a6aa42f fix: add main entry point to wrangler.toml for SPA fallback worker
wrangler 4.x rejects binding = "ASSETS" on assets-only Workers.
Adding main = "worker.mjs" tells wrangler there is a Worker script,
enabling env.ASSETS and the /link SPA fallback route.

content.content: /Users/agada/openclaw-ecosystem-wallet-skill/polygon-agent-kit/lib/polymarket.mjs
/Users/agada/openclaw-ecosystem-wallet-skill/polygon-agent-kit/cli/commands/polymarket.mjs

1→# Polymarket CLI — Implementation Notes & Debugging Log
     2→
     3→This document captures every bug, root cause, and fix discovered while building and testing the Polymarket CLI integration end-to-end. It serves as a reference for anyone extending or debugging this code.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Architecture Summary
     8→
     9→```
    10→Sequence ecosystem smart wallet (USDC.e)
    11→        ↓  fund (USDC.e transfer)
    12→Builder EOA (0x1218...)
    13→        ↓  signs everything
    14→  ├── on-chain: approve → split → mints YES+NO tokens
    15→  └── CLOB API: EIP-712 L1 auth → HMAC L2 → post SELL order
    16→```
    17→
    18→The smart wallet cannot sign Polymarket payloads — it uses a multi-sig scheme incompatible with Polymarket's EIP-712 and HMAC auth. All signing must go through the builder EOA (a regular private-key account).
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## Files
    23→
    24→| File | Purpose |
    25→|------|---------|
    26→| `lib/polymarket.mjs` | Gamma API, CLOB auth, on-chain viem ops |
    27→| `cli/commands/polymarket.mjs` | 6 CLI commands wiring everything together |
    28→| `skills/POLYMARKET.md` | User-facing command reference |
    29→
    30→---
    31→
    32→## Bug Log
    33→
    34→### Bug 1 — Gamma API search param silently ignored
    35→
    36→**Symptom:** `--search bitcoin` returned all markets, not filtered ones.
    37→
    38→**Root cause:** The Gamma API ignores unknown/unsupported query parameters without error. There is no server-side full-text search.
    39→
    40→**Fix:** Fetch a larger result set (5× limit when search is set, minimum 100) and filter client-side with a case-insensitive substring match on `question`.
    41→
    42→---
    43→
    44→### Bug 2 — `getMarket(conditionId)` returned wrong market
    45→
    46→**Symptom:** Passing `?conditionId=0xabc...` to the Gamma API returned unrelated markets.
    47→
    48→**Root cause:** The Gamma API silently ignores the `conditionId` query parameter.
    49→
    50→**Fix:** Scan up to 500 markets in pages of 100, find the market by exact `conditionId` match on the client side.
    51→
    52→---
    53→
    54→### Bug 3 — CLOB L1 auth returning 401 "Invalid L1 Request headers"
    55→
    56→**Symptom:** `POST /auth/api-key` returned 401 on every attempt.
    57→
    58→**Root cause (part 1):** Header names used dashes (`POLY-ADDRESS`) instead of underscores (`POLY_ADDRESS`).
    59→
    60→**Root cause (part 2):** The EIP-712 `ClobAuth` struct field `address` was typed as `"string"` in the types definition. Polymarket's contract expects it as `"address"` so the typed-data hash is different.
    61→
    62→```javascript
    63→// WRONG
    64→{ name: 'address', type: 'string' }
    65→
    66→// CORRECT
    67→{ name: 'address', type: 'address' }
    68→```
    69→
    70→**Fix:** Changed header names to underscores; changed field type to `"address"`.
    71→
    72→---
    73→
    74→### Bug 4 — HMAC signature mismatch
    75→
    76→**Symptom:** CLOB authenticated endpoints (`/data/orders`) returned auth errors.
    77→
    78→**Root cause:** The CLOB secret is base64-URL encoded. It must be decoded before use as an HMAC key, and the output digest must also be base64-URL encoded (not standard base64).
    79→
    80→```javascript
    81→// WRONG
    82→const keyBuf = Buffer.from(creds.secret, 'base64')
    83→const sig = hmac.digest('base64')
    84→
    85→// CORRECT
    86→const keyBuf = Buffer.from(creds.secret, 'base64url')
    87→const sig = hmac.digest('base64url')
    88→```
    89→
    90→---
    91→
    92→### Bug 5 — Open orders endpoint returning 405
    93→
    94→**Symptom:** `polymarket orders` got HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed.
    95→
    96→**Root cause:** The orders endpoint is `/data/orders`, not `/orders`.
    97→
    98→**Fix:** Updated endpoint path in `getOpenOrders()`.
    99→
   100→---
   101→
   102→### Bug 6 — CLOB 400 "Could not create api key"
   103→
   104→**Symptom:** `deriveClobCreds()` always returned 400 even with correct auth headers.
   105→
   106→**Root cause:** This is a Polymarket account gate, not a code bug. The builder EOA (`0x1218...`) must have accepted Polymarket's Terms of Service at least once on polymarket.com before the CLOB will issue API credentials.
   107→
   108→**Fix:** Not a code fix. The EOA owner must visit polymarket.com, connect the EOA wallet, and accept terms. After that, `POST /auth/api-key` succeeds.
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→### Bug 7 — viem `sendTransaction` failing with 401 on `polygon-rpc.com`
   113→
   114→**Symptom:** The `approveUsdce` step failed: `HTTP 401 — API key disabled, reason: tenant disabled`.
   115→
   116→**Root cause:** viem's `http()` transport with no argument defaults to `https://polygon-rpc.com/` which requires a proprietary API key that the free tier no longer supports.
   117→
   118→**Fix (temporary during testing):** Pointed viem at a working RPC. This was later reverted — the correct long-term fix is to configure a Polygon RPC via the `POLYGON_RPC_URL` environment variable or use the Sequence nodes URL derived from the project access key.
   119→
   120→**Current state:** `http()` is back to viem default. If you hit 401 on polygon-rpc.com, set:
   121→```bash
   122→export POLYGON_RPC_URL=<your-rpc-url>
   123→```
   124→and update the `http()` calls in `lib/polymarket.mjs` (2 places) and `cli/commands/polymarket.mjs` (2 places) to `http(process.env.POLYGON_RPC_URL)`.
   125→
   126→---
   127→
   128→### Bug 8 — `splitPosition` reverting on neg-risk markets
   129→
   130→**Symptom:** Calling `CTF.splitPosition(USDC.e, bytes32(0), conditionId, [1,2], amount)` reverted on neg-risk markets.
   131→
   132→**Root cause:** Neg-risk markets use a different contract architecture. The standard CTF split doesn't work because the neg-risk market's condition was registered through the **Neg Risk Adapter**, which uses a wrapped collateral (`wcol`) internally. Calling CTF directly bypasses this wrapping step.
   133→
   134→**Fix:** For neg-risk markets, call `NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition(bytes32 conditionId, uint256 amount)` instead. This is the simplified 2-argument overload on the adapter that handles wrapping internally.
   135→
   136→```javascript
   137→// Regular market
   138→CTF.splitPosition(USDC_E, bytes32(0), conditionId, [1n, 2n], amount)
   139→
   140→// Neg-risk market
   141→NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition(conditionId, amount)
   142→```
   143→
   144→The `negRisk` flag is read from the Gamma API market data and passed through to `splitPosition()`.
   145→
   146→---
   147→
   148→### Bug 9 — USDC.e approval going to wrong target for neg-risk
   149→
   150→**Symptom:** Even after fixing the split contract target, the split still reverted.
   151→
   152→**Root cause:** The `approveUsdce` step approved the **CTF Exchange** as the spender. But for neg-risk markets, the **Neg Risk Adapter** pulls the USDC.e (to wrap it). The adapter had no allowance.
   153→
   154→**Fix:** For neg-risk markets, approve USDC.e → Neg Risk Adapter instead of CTF Exchange.
   155→
   156→```javascript
   157→const usdceApproveTarget = market.negRisk ? NEG_RISK_ADAPTER : CTF_EXCHANGE
   158→await approveUsdce(walletClient, publicClient, usdceApproveTarget, MAX_UINT256)
   159→```
   160→
   161→---
   162→
   163→### Bug 10 — EOA had no POL for gas
   164→
   165→**Symptom:** After funding with USDC.e, approve transactions reverted with `insufficient funds for gas * price + value`.
   166→
   167→**Root cause:** The builder EOA receives USDC.e from the smart wallet but has zero native POL. On Polygon, EOA transactions (approve, split) require native POL for gas. The smart wallet pays its own gas via the Sequence relayer (which accepts USDC.e as fee), but EOA transactions go directly to the network.
   168→
   169→**Fix:** Before using the EOA for on-chain ops, send a small amount of POL to it:
   170→
   171→```bash
   172→node cli/polygon-agent.mjs send-native \
   173→  --to 0x1218807100A12caA9AFcc0Bd9852E9bECf2FBa77 \
   174→  --amount 0.1 \
   175→  --broadcast
   176→```
   177→
   178→0.1 POL covers many approve + split transactions. The `polymarket buy` flow now documents this requirement in the troubleshooting table.
   179→
   180→---
   181→
   182→### Feature — `--skip-fund` flag
   183→
   184→During testing the funding step succeeded but subsequent steps failed, leaving USDC.e stranded in the EOA. Re-running `buy` would try to fund again (double-spend from smart wallet).
   185→
   186→**Fix:** Added `--skip-fund` flag that skips the smart wallet → EOA transfer and uses the EOA's existing balance directly.
   187→
   188→```bash
   189→# Use EOA's existing USDC.e balance — skip the smart wallet funding step
   190→node cli/polygon-agent.mjs polymarket buy <conditionId> YES 0.2 --broadcast --skip-fund
   191→```
   192→
   193→---
   194→
   195→## Confirmed Working Live Flow (2026-02-24)
   196→
   197→1. Smart wallet (`0xc2cf50...`) sent 0.2 USDC.e to builder EOA (`0x1218...`)
   198→   - txHash: `0xcd943e8aba1f61bff3b27994d71389c47ab1f320b4304897920fb70bcf3395b5`
   199→
   200→2. Smart wallet sent 0.1 POL to EOA for gas
   201→   - txHash: `0xa53399a3141c199fd2d73247afcfafbb37eff49bcd90008476868d08f02dba7c`
   202→
   203→3. EOA approved USDC.e → Neg Risk Adapter (max uint256)
   204→   - txHash: `0x361f987a2179ff5097afc97e07e0d4f7c4b29d43732f4cf80d292155c6e810a6`
   205→
   206→4. EOA set CTF approval for CTF Exchange
   207→   - txHash: `0xda5075be677a5f593fdcc016c141f22b302d589371bc2d0019e4eff13a16907d`
   208→
   209→5. EOA set CTF approvals for Neg Risk Exchange + Adapter
   210→   - txHash: `0x01a26bd754b317d0a1457d7eb6986dd00ec2ade60a1fcaf412a3999f1c4c2bb1`
   211→
   212→6. **EOA called NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition → minted YES + NO tokens**
   213→   - txHash: `0x65153ae34acacee8d6757a2b94a982ce8e29b2b8313736c47abf8f4bc9aa09d4`
   214→
   215→7. CLOB sell order for NO tokens — **pending** (EOA needs Polymarket account registration)
   216→
   217→**Final on-chain state (confirmed):**
   218→```
   219→EOA 0x1218... holds:
   220→  YES: 200,000 tokens ($0.20 worth)
   221→  NO:  200,000 tokens ($0.20 worth)
   222→
   223→Market: "Will Anthropic have the best AI model at the end of February 2026?"
   224→```
   225→
   226→---
   227→
   228→## Neg Risk Market Contract Flow (detailed)
   229→
   230→For neg-risk markets, the token architecture differs from standard markets:
   231→
   232→```
   233→USDC.e → NegRiskAdapter → wraps to wcol
   234→wcol → CTF.splitPosition → YES tokens + NO tokens
   235→YES tokens: held in EOA (wanted outcome)
   236→NO tokens:  held in EOA (sell via CLOB)
   237→```
   238→
   239→Required approvals (neg-risk):
   240→1. `USDC.e.approve(NegRiskAdapter, MAX)` — adapter pulls USDC.e to wrap
   241→2. `CTF.setApprovalForAll(CTF_Exchange, true)` — CLOB exchange can transfer tokens
   242→3. `CTF.setApprovalForAll(NegRiskCTFExchange, true)` — neg risk exchange can transfer
   243→4. `CTF.setApprovalForAll(NegRiskAdapter, true)` — adapter can operate on tokens
   244→
   245→---
   246→
   247→## EOA Gas Requirement
   248→
   249→The builder EOA needs **native POL** on Polygon to pay gas for:
   250→- `USDC.e.approve(...)` — ~50k gas
   251→- `CTF.setApprovalForAll(...)` — ~50k gas each (up to 3 calls for neg-risk)
   252→- `NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition(...)` or `CTF.splitPosition(...)` — ~150–300k gas
   253→
   254→At typical Polygon gas prices (~30 gwei), 0.05–0.1 POL covers a full buy cycle. Subsequent buys can reuse existing approvals and only need gas for the split (~0.01 POL).
   255→
   256→---
   257→
   258→## CLOB Account Requirement
   259→
   260→`polymarket orders` and `polymarket cancel` require the EOA to have a registered Polymarket CLOB account. The `deriveClobCreds()` call (`POST /auth/api-key`) returns 400 until the EOA has accepted Polymarket's Terms of Service on polymarket.com.
   261→
   262→The `polymarket buy` command handles this gracefully: if CLOB creds fail, it reports that the split succeeded and tokens are held in the EOA, with instructions to sell manually.
   263→

# Polymarket CLI — Implementation Notes & Debugging Log

This document captures every bug, root cause, and fix discovered while building and testing the Polymarket CLI integration end-to-end. It serves as a reference for anyone extending or debugging this code.

---

## Architecture Summary

```
Sequence ecosystem smart wallet (USDC.e)
        ↓  fund (USDC.e transfer)
Builder EOA (0x1218...)
        ↓  signs everything
  ├── on-chain: approve → split → mints YES+NO tokens
  └── CLOB API: EIP-712 L1 auth → HMAC L2 → post SELL order
```

The smart wallet cannot sign Polymarket payloads — it uses a multi-sig scheme incompatible with Polymarket's EIP-712 and HMAC auth. All signing must go through the builder EOA (a regular private-key account).

---

## Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `lib/polymarket.mjs` | Gamma API, CLOB auth, on-chain viem ops |
| `cli/commands/polymarket.mjs` | 6 CLI commands wiring everything together |
| `skills/POLYMARKET.md` | User-facing command reference |

---

## Bug Log

### Bug 1 — Gamma API search param silently ignored

**Symptom:** `--search bitcoin` returned all markets, not filtered ones.

**Root cause:** The Gamma API ignores unknown/unsupported query parameters without error. There is no server-side full-text search.

**Fix:** Fetch a larger result set (5× limit when search is set, minimum 100) and filter client-side with a case-insensitive substring match on `question`.

---

### Bug 2 — `getMarket(conditionId)` returned wrong market

**Symptom:** Passing `?conditionId=0xabc...` to the Gamma API returned unrelated markets.

**Root cause:** The Gamma API silently ignores the `conditionId` query parameter.

**Fix:** Scan up to 500 markets in pages of 100, find the market by exact `conditionId` match on the client side.

---

### Bug 3 — CLOB L1 auth returning 401 "Invalid L1 Request headers"

**Symptom:** `POST /auth/api-key` returned 401 on every attempt.

**Root cause (part 1):** Header names used dashes (`POLY-ADDRESS`) instead of underscores (`POLY_ADDRESS`).

**Root cause (part 2):** The EIP-712 `ClobAuth` struct field `address` was typed as `"string"` in the types definition. Polymarket's contract expects it as `"address"` so the typed-data hash is different.

```javascript
// WRONG
{ name: 'address', type: 'string' }

// CORRECT
{ name: 'address', type: 'address' }
```

**Fix:** Changed header names to underscores; changed field type to `"address"`.

---

### Bug 4 — HMAC signature mismatch

**Symptom:** CLOB authenticated endpoints (`/data/orders`) returned auth errors.

**Root cause:** The CLOB secret is base64-URL encoded. It must be decoded before use as an HMAC key, and the output digest must also be base64-URL encoded (not standard base64).

```javascript
// WRONG
const keyBuf = Buffer.from(creds.secret, 'base64')
const sig = hmac.digest('base64')

// CORRECT
const keyBuf = Buffer.from(creds.secret, 'base64url')
const sig = hmac.digest('base64url')
```

---

### Bug 5 — Open orders endpoint returning 405

**Symptom:** `polymarket orders` got HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed.

**Root cause:** The orders endpoint is `/data/orders`, not `/orders`.

**Fix:** Updated endpoint path in `getOpenOrders()`.

---

### Bug 6 — CLOB 400 "Could not create api key"

**Symptom:** `deriveClobCreds()` always returned 400 even with correct auth headers.

**Root cause:** This is a Polymarket account gate, not a code bug. The builder EOA (`0x1218...`) must have accepted Polymarket's Terms of Service at least once on polymarket.com before the CLOB will issue API credentials.

**Fix:** Not a code fix. The EOA owner must visit polymarket.com, connect the EOA wallet, and accept terms. After that, `POST /auth/api-key` succeeds.

---

### Bug 7 — viem `sendTransaction` failing with 401 on `polygon-rpc.com`

**Symptom:** The `approveUsdce` step failed: `HTTP 401 — API key disabled, reason: tenant disabled`.

**Root cause:** viem's `http()` transport with no argument defaults to `https://polygon-rpc.com/` which requires a proprietary API key that the free tier no longer supports.

**Fix (temporary during testing):** Pointed viem at a working RPC. This was later reverted — the correct long-term fix is to configure a Polygon RPC via the `POLYGON_RPC_URL` environment variable or use the Sequence nodes URL derived from the project access key.

**Current state:** `http()` is back to viem default. If you hit 401 on polygon-rpc.com, set:
```bash
export POLYGON_RPC_URL=<your-rpc-url>
```
and update the `http()` calls in `lib/polymarket.mjs` (2 places) and `cli/commands/polymarket.mjs` (2 places) to `http(process.env.POLYGON_RPC_URL)`.

---

### Bug 8 — `splitPosition` reverting on neg-risk markets

**Symptom:** Calling `CTF.splitPosition(USDC.e, bytes32(0), conditionId, [1,2], amount)` reverted on neg-risk markets.

**Root cause:** Neg-risk markets use a different contract architecture. The standard CTF split doesn't work because the neg-risk market's condition was registered through the **Neg Risk Adapter**, which uses a wrapped collateral (`wcol`) internally. Calling CTF directly bypasses this wrapping step.

**Fix:** For neg-risk markets, call `NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition(bytes32 conditionId, uint256 amount)` instead. This is the simplified 2-argument overload on the adapter that handles wrapping internally.

```javascript
// Regular market
CTF.splitPosition(USDC_E, bytes32(0), conditionId, [1n, 2n], amount)

// Neg-risk market
NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition(conditionId, amount)
```

The `negRisk` flag is read from the Gamma API market data and passed through to `splitPosition()`.

---

### Bug 9 — USDC.e approval going to wrong target for neg-risk

**Symptom:** Even after fixing the split contract target, the split still reverted.

**Root cause:** The `approveUsdce` step approved the **CTF Exchange** as the spender. But for neg-risk markets, the **Neg Risk Adapter** pulls the USDC.e (to wrap it). The adapter had no allowance.

**Fix:** For neg-risk markets, approve USDC.e → Neg Risk Adapter instead of CTF Exchange.

```javascript
const usdceApproveTarget = market.negRisk ? NEG_RISK_ADAPTER : CTF_EXCHANGE
await approveUsdce(walletClient, publicClient, usdceApproveTarget, MAX_UINT256)
```

---

### Bug 10 — EOA had no POL for gas

**Symptom:** After funding with USDC.e, approve transactions reverted with `insufficient funds for gas * price + value`.

**Root cause:** The builder EOA receives USDC.e from the smart wallet but has zero native POL. On Polygon, EOA transactions (approve, split) require native POL for gas. The smart wallet pays its own gas via the Sequence relayer (which accepts USDC.e as fee), but EOA transactions go directly to the network.

**Fix:** Before using the EOA for on-chain ops, send a small amount of POL to it:

```bash
node cli/polygon-agent.mjs send-native \
  --to 0x1218807100A12caA9AFcc0Bd9852E9bECf2FBa77 \
  --amount 0.1 \
  --broadcast
```

0.1 POL covers many approve + split transactions. The `polymarket buy` flow now documents this requirement in the troubleshooting table.

---

### Feature — `--skip-fund` flag

During testing the funding step succeeded but subsequent steps failed, leaving USDC.e stranded in the EOA. Re-running `buy` would try to fund again (double-spend from smart wallet).

**Fix:** Added `--skip-fund` flag that skips the smart wallet → EOA transfer and uses the EOA's existing balance directly.

```bash
# Use EOA's existing USDC.e balance — skip the smart wallet funding step
node cli/polygon-agent.mjs polymarket buy <conditionId> YES 0.2 --broadcast --skip-fund
```

---

## Confirmed Working Live Flow (2026-02-24)

1. Smart wallet (`0xc2cf50...`) sent 0.2 USDC.e to builder EOA (`0x1218...`)
   - txHash: `0xcd943e8aba1f61bff3b27994d71389c47ab1f320b4304897920fb70bcf3395b5`

2. Smart wallet sent 0.1 POL to EOA for gas
   - txHash: `0xa53399a3141c199fd2d73247afcfafbb37eff49bcd90008476868d08f02dba7c`

3. EOA approved USDC.e → Neg Risk Adapter (max uint256)
   - txHash: `0x361f987a2179ff5097afc97e07e0d4f7c4b29d43732f4cf80d292155c6e810a6`

4. EOA set CTF approval for CTF Exchange
   - txHash: `0xda5075be677a5f593fdcc016c141f22b302d589371bc2d0019e4eff13a16907d`

5. EOA set CTF approvals for Neg Risk Exchange + Adapter
   - txHash: `0x01a26bd754b317d0a1457d7eb6986dd00ec2ade60a1fcaf412a3999f1c4c2bb1`

6. **EOA called NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition → minted YES + NO tokens**
   - txHash: `0x65153ae34acacee8d6757a2b94a982ce8e29b2b8313736c47abf8f4bc9aa09d4`

7. CLOB sell order for NO tokens — **pending** (EOA needs Polymarket account registration)

**Final on-chain state (confirmed):**
```
EOA 0x1218... holds:
  YES: 200,000 tokens ($0.20 worth)
  NO:  200,000 tokens ($0.20 worth)

Market: "Will Anthropic have the best AI model at the end of February 2026?"
```

---

## Neg Risk Market Contract Flow (detailed)

For neg-risk markets, the token architecture differs from standard markets:

```
USDC.e → NegRiskAdapter → wraps to wcol
wcol → CTF.splitPosition → YES tokens + NO tokens
YES tokens: held in EOA (wanted outcome)
NO tokens:  held in EOA (sell via CLOB)
```

Required approvals (neg-risk):
1. `USDC.e.approve(NegRiskAdapter, MAX)` — adapter pulls USDC.e to wrap
2. `CTF.setApprovalForAll(CTF_Exchange, true)` — CLOB exchange can transfer tokens
3. `CTF.setApprovalForAll(NegRiskCTFExchange, true)` — neg risk exchange can transfer
4. `CTF.setApprovalForAll(NegRiskAdapter, true)` — adapter can operate on tokens

---

## EOA Gas Requirement

The builder EOA needs **native POL** on Polygon to pay gas for:
- `USDC.e.approve(...)` — ~50k gas
- `CTF.setApprovalForAll(...)` — ~50k gas each (up to 3 calls for neg-risk)
- `NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition(...)` or `CTF.splitPosition(...)` — ~150–300k gas

At typical Polygon gas prices (~30 gwei), 0.05–0.1 POL covers a full buy cycle. Subsequent buys can reuse existing approvals and only need gas for the split (~0.01 POL).

---

## CLOB Account Requirement

`polymarket orders` and `polymarket cancel` require the EOA to have a registered Polymarket CLOB account. The `deriveClobCreds()` call (`POST /auth/api-key`) returns 400 until the EOA has accepted Polymarket's Terms of Service on polymarket.com.

The `polymarket buy` command handles this gracefully: if CLOB creds fail, it reports that the split succeeded and tokens are held in the EOA, with instructions to sell manually.

content.text:
Here's my full understanding of the project:

---

## Project Overview

**Polygon Agent Kit** — An end-to-end blockchain toolkit for AI agents on Polygon. It gives agents wallets, token operations, DeFi, on-chain identity, and prediction market access through a single CLI entry point (`polygon-agent`).

### Architecture Pillars

| Layer | Technology | What it does |
|-------|-----------|--------------|
| **Wallets** | Sequence | Session-based smart contract wallets (AA) with scoped spending permissions |
| **DeFi** | Trails | Swaps, bridges, deposits (Aave/Morpho), cross-chain ops |
| **Identity** | ERC-8004 (Polygon mainnet) | Agent registry as ERC-721 NFTs with reputation |
| **Payments** | x402 | HTTP-native micropayments between agents |
| **Prediction Markets** | Polymarket | Buy YES/NO tokens via CLOB |

### Two-Wallet Pattern
- **EOA** (`builder.json`) — created by `setup`, used for signing CLOB auth (EIP-712), on-chain Polymarket ops (approve/split), gas-paying txs. Never the primary funds wallet.
- **Ecosystem Smart Wallet** — created by `wallet create`, primary spending wallet, uses Sequence relayer for gas abstraction (pays fees in USDC).

---

## Current Branch: `feature/polymarket`

You're building **Polymarket prediction market integration** — 6 new CLI commands under `polygon-agent polymarket`:

| Command | What it does |
|---------|-------------|
| `polymarket markets` | List/search active markets from Gamma API |
| `polymarket market <conditionId>` | Get single market details |
| `polymarket buy <conditionId> YES\|NO <amount>` | Full buy flow (split → CLOB sell) |
| `polymarket positions` | Open positions in smart wallet |
| `polymarket orders` | Open CLOB orders for builder EOA |
| `polymarket cancel <orderId>` | Cancel a CLOB order |

### Buy Flow Architecture

```
Sequence smart wallet (USDC.e)
        ↓  send-token transfer
Builder EOA (0x1218...)
        ↓  signs everything (needs ~0.1 POL for gas)
  ├── approve USDC.e → CTF Exchange (or Neg Risk Adapter)
  ├── setApprovalForAll CTF (up to 3 calls for neg-risk)
  ├── splitPosition → mints YES + NO tokens
  └── CLOB SELL order for unwanted side (HMAC auth)
```

### Key Design Decisions
- Smart wallet **cannot** sign Polymarket payloads (multi-sig incompatible with EIP-712/HMAC), so all signing goes through the EOA
- Sequence relayer handles smart wallet gas (accepts USDC), but EOA txs need native POL
- `--skip-fund` flag lets you retry from EOA's existing balance after partial failures

### Commit History on This Branch (6 commits)
1. **`acdcb58`** `feat(polymarket)` — Initial implementation: `lib/polymarket.mjs` + `cli/commands/polymarket.mjs`, 6 commands
2. **`a67a9cb`** `fix(polymarket)` — Fixed CLOB L1 auth (EIP-712 field types, header underscore names), fixed search (client-side filter), fixed orders endpoint (`/data/orders`)
3. **`2b157e3`** `docs(polymarket)` — Added `POLYMARKET.md` full reference
4. **`43b3547`** `fix(polymarket)` — Neg-risk markets: correct `NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition()`, correct USDC.e approval target, `--skip-fund` flag. **Confirmed working live 2026-02-24.**
5. **`01aa9d3`** `docs(polymarket)` — Added `polymarket-skill.md` compact agent reference + POL gas docs
6. **`17932dd`** `fix(polymarket)` — Fixed CLOB auth: fallback from POST to GET `/auth/derive-api-key` when keys exist, fixed HMAC base64 encoding (preserves `=` padding per Polymarket server validation)

### Untracked files (not yet committed)
- `skills/POLYMARKET_IMPLEMENTATION.md` — debugging log with all 10 bugs found and fixed
- `test-smart-wallet-polymarket.mjs` — test script
- `agents.md`, `imp.txt` — notes

The feature is functionally complete — the full buy flow has been confirmed working on-chain (live txns from 2026-02-24). The remaining gap is CLOB sell orders requiring the EOA to have a registered Polymarket account (accept ToS at polymarket.com).

content.content:
17932ddb855ec30d10266e0d9fda5bc0c3075f79 fix(polymarket): fix CLOB auth — derive fallback + correct HMAC encoding
Three bugs fixed:
- POST /auth/api-key returns 400 if keys already exist; fall back to
  GET /auth/derive-api-key to retrieve existing credentials
- Map apiKey field correctly: creds.apiKey || creds.key
- HMAC digest was using Node base64url (strips = padding) instead of
  the TS clob-client approach: base64 + manual +→- /→_ substitution
  (preserves = padding), which is what Polymarket's server validates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: d03f1775c678

01aa9d31c9c0cdbdd812c7df729b6b2771aa85c5 docs(polymarket): add POL gas requirement + compact skill file
POLYMARKET.md:
- Add POL gas prerequisite with exact send-native command
- Add two new troubleshooting rows: insufficient gas, skip-fund retry
- Add "Why EOA Needs POL" section explaining x402-pay vs Polymarket difference

polymarket-skill.md (new):
- Compact, context-window-efficient reference — no repetition
- Covers setup, all commands, buy flow, neg risk, flags, errors, contracts

43b3547f7d181c9be724853cfc037194f9bcaff8 fix(polymarket): neg risk split, EOA gas, skip-fund flag + implementation docs
On-chain fixes (confirmed working live 2026-02-24):
- splitPosition for neg-risk markets now calls NegRiskAdapter.splitPosition(bytes32,uint256)
  instead of CTF.splitPosition — neg risk conditions must go through the adapter
- USDC.e approval target for neg-risk markets is NegRiskAdapter (not CTF Exchange);
  the adapter wraps USDC.e to wcol before calling CTF internally
- Pass negRisk flag from Gamma market data through to splitPosition()

UX:
- Add --skip-fund flag to polymarket buy: skips smart wallet→EOA transfer and uses
  EOA's existing USDC.e balance — avoids double-spend when retrying after partial failure

Covers all 6 CLI commands with options, output fields, and examples.
Includes step-by-step flow from setup → market discovery → dry-run →
broadcast → check position → cancel, plus troubleshooting table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

a67a9cb7f1e09fa2fe1898ead3d46202d6c300bd fix(polymarket): correct CLOB auth, search, and orders endpoint
- L1 auth: fix EIP-712 field types (address not string), header names
  use underscores (POLY_ADDRESS) per py-clob-client spec
- L2 HMAC: base64-URL decode secret before signing; base64-URL encode
  output; use underscore header names
- GET orders: fix endpoint /orders → /data/orders
- getMarkets: client-side search filter (Gamma has no server-side FTS)
- getMarket: scan top markets to find by conditionId (Gamma conditionId
  param is ignored server-side)

Note: POST /auth/api-key returns 400 for EOAs with no Polymarket
account history — address must have interacted with Polymarket first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: 9f869c12047d

acdcb582e91f9f1cd9e2eb2b9043ef98a4445e49 feat(polymarket): add Polymarket prediction market operations
Adds 6 new CLI commands under `polygon-agent polymarket`:
- markets  — list active markets from Gamma API
- market   — get single market by conditionId
- buy      — split USDC.e → YES/NO tokens, sell unwanted side on CLOB
- positions — open positions for smart wallet (Data API)
- orders   — open CLOB orders for builder EOA
- cancel   — cancel a CLOB order by ID

Architecture follows x402-pay pattern: smart wallet funds builder EOA,
EOA handles all on-chain (approve, splitPosition) and CLOB signing
(HMAC-SHA256 auth, EIP-712 order structs via viem signTypedData).

New files:
- lib/polymarket.mjs — Gamma/CLOB/Data API clients + viem on-chain ops
- cli/commands/polymarket.mjs — 6 command functions

No new npm dependencies (viem already installed, crypto is Node built-in).

0d6ac49ab5b8a065081e0182d1625793c3beaae1 Merge pull request #17 from 0xPolygon/fix/fee-selection-prefer-usdc
fix: prefer ERC20 (USDC) fee over native POL when wallet has no POL
2877a9484d11f2d1d8390d3bcba7ce29ef57a9ae fix: prefer ERC20 (USDC) fee over native POL when wallet has no POL
- Fix broken native fee detection: zero address "0x000...000" is a
  truthy string, so !o?.token?.contractAddress was always false for
  native POL option; switched to explicit zero-address check
- Remove dangerous fallback to feeOptions[0] (often POL) when native
  not found — this caused SimulationFailed when wallet had no POL
- Change sendToken to preferNativeFee: false so ERC20 sends always
  use the ERC20 fee path (getFeeTokens → USDC), not simulation-based
  getFeeOptions which can return POL as first option

657abc3f58261d09fa5bef975ddba80737d033d9 Merge pull request #16 from 0xPolygon/fix/ngrok-no-authtoken-required
fix: ngrok tunnel works without NGROK_AUTHTOKEN env var
e44a978386cbdcba963ea83ab5da1fc96d675856 fix: ngrok tunnel works without NGROK_AUTHTOKEN env var
authtoken_from_env: true forced the SDK to only look at the env var,
causing a silent fallback to localhost when it wasn't set. Now the SDK
resolves auth from the local ngrok config (~/.ngrok2/ngrok.yml) by
default, with NGROK_AUTHTOKEN as an explicit override — matching how
the system ngrok binary behaves.

wrangler 4.x rejects binding = "ASSETS" on assets-only Workers.
Adding main = "worker.mjs" tells wrangler there is a Worker script,
enabling env.ASSETS and the /link SPA fallback route.

content.text:
Here is the complete analysis of the `polymarket-cli` repository:

---

## Repository Overview

**Language:** Rust (2024 edition)  
**Binary name:** `polymarket`  
**Version:** 0.1.4  
**License:** MIT  
**Repo:** https://github.com/Polymarket/polymarket-cli

This is a Rust CLI for Polymarket — a prediction market platform on Polygon (chain ID 137). It supports browsing markets, placing orders via the CLOB API, managing wallets, and interacting directly with on-chain contracts.

---

## File Structure

```
/tmp/polymarket-cli/
├── Cargo.toml
├── Cargo.lock
├── README.md
├── install.sh
├── Formula/polymarket.rb
├── scripts/update-formula.sh
├── .github/workflows/ci.yml
├── src/
│   ├── main.rs          -- Entry point, clap CLI parsing
│   ├── auth.rs          -- Wallet resolution, RPC, CLOB authentication
│   ├── config.rs        -- Config file (~/.config/polymarket/config.json)
│   ├── shell.rs         -- Interactive REPL (rustyline)
│   ├── commands/
│   │   ├── mod.rs       -- Shared helpers (is_numeric_id, parse_address)
│   │   ├── clob.rs      -- All CLOB subcommands (1178 lines)
│   │   ├── markets.rs   -- Market listing/search/get
│   │   ├── events.rs    -- Event listing/get
│   │   ├── approve.rs   -- ERC-20/ERC-1155 contract approvals
│   │   ├── ctf.rs       -- Split/merge/redeem conditional tokens
│   │   ├── wallet.rs    -- Create/import/show/reset wallet
│   │   ├── data.rs      -- On-chain portfolio, trades, leaderboard
│   │   ├── bridge.rs    -- Cross-chain deposit support
│   │   ├── setup.rs     -- Interactive first-time setup wizard
│   │   ├── tags.rs      -- Tag lookup/related
│   │   ├── series.rs    -- Series listing/get
│   │   ├── comments.rs  -- Comment listing by entity or user
│   │   ├── profiles.rs  -- Public profile lookup
│   │   ├── sports.rs    -- Sports metadata/teams
│   │   └── upgrade.rs   -- Self-update via GitHub releases
│   └── output/
│       ├── mod.rs       -- OutputFormat enum, shared helpers
│       ├── clob.rs      -- CLOB table/JSON renderers (1467 lines)
│       ├── markets.rs   -- Market table rendering
│       ├── events.rs    -- Event table rendering
│       ├── approve.rs   -- Approval status rendering
│       ├── ctf.rs       -- CTF tx result rendering
│       ├── data.rs      -- Portfolio, trades, leaderboard rendering
│       ├── bridge.rs    -- Bridge status rendering
│       ├── comments.rs
│       ├── profiles.rs
│       ├── series.rs
│       ├── sports.rs
│       └── tags.rs
└── tests/cli_integration.rs  -- Integration tests via assert_cmd
```

---

## `/tmp/polymarket-cli/Cargo.toml`

```toml
[package]
name = "polymarket-cli"
version = "0.1.4"
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.88.0"
description = "CLI for Polymarket — browse markets, trade, and manage positions"
license = "MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/Polymarket/polymarket-cli"
keywords = ["polymarket", "prediction-markets", "trading", "cli"]
categories = ["command-line-utilities"]

[[bin]]
name = "polymarket"
path = "src/main.rs"

[dependencies]
polymarket-client-sdk = { version = "0.4", features = ["gamma", "data", "bridge", "clob", "ctf"] }
alloy = { version = "1.6.3", default-features = false, features = ["providers", "sol-types", "contract", "reqwest", "reqwest-rustls-tls", "signer-local", "signers"] }
clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros"] }
serde_json = "1"
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
tabled = "0.17"
rust_decimal = "1"
anyhow = "1"
chrono = "0.4"
dirs = "6"
rustyline = "15"

[dev-dependencies]
assert_cmd = "2"
predicates = "3"
rust_decimal_macros = "1"

[profile.release]
lto = "thin"
codegen-units = 1
strip = true
panic = "abort"
```

**Key dependencies:**
- `polymarket-client-sdk 0.4` — First-party Polymarket Rust SDK (features: gamma, data, bridge, clob, ctf)
- `alloy 1.6.3` — Ethereum/EVM client (RPC, signing, contract calls)
- `clap 4` — CLI argument parsing
- `tokio 1` — Async runtime
- `tabled 0.17` — Table rendering
- `rustyline 15` — Interactive REPL with history
- `rust_decimal 1` — Precision decimal arithmetic for prices/amounts
- `anyhow 1` — Error handling

---

## `src/main.rs`

Full top-level CLI structure. All subcommand routing happens in `run()`.

**Top-level commands:**
- `setup` — Guided first-time wizard
- `shell` — Interactive REPL
- `markets` — Market browse/search
- `events` — Event browse
- `tags` — Tag lookup
- `series` — Series listing
- `comments` — Comment listing
- `profiles` — Public profiles
- `sports` — Sports metadata
- `approve` — Contract approvals (ERC-20/ERC-1155)
- `clob` — Full CLOB trading API
- `ctf` — CTF split/merge/redeem
- `data` — On-chain portfolio/trades
- `bridge` — Cross-chain deposit
- `wallet` — Key management
- `status` — API health check
- `upgrade` — Self-update

Global flags: `--output table|json` (short: `-o`), `--private-key`, `--signature-type`.

---

## `src/auth.rs`

```rust
pub const RPC_URL: &str = "https://polygon.drpc.org";
```

**Key functions:**

### `resolve_signer(private_key: Option<&str>) -> Result<impl Signer>`
Resolves a `LocalSigner` from CLI flag → env var → config file (via `config::resolve_key`). The signer is bound to `POLYGON` chain ID (137).

### `authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type_flag) -> Result<clob::Client<Authenticated<Normal>>>`
The main authenticated CLOB client factory:
1. Calls `resolve_signer` to get a `LocalSigner`
2. Calls `authenticate_with_signer`

### `authenticate_with_signer(signer, signature_type_flag) -> Result<clob::Client<Authenticated<Normal>>>`
Uses the SDK's builder pattern:
```rust
clob::Client::default()
    .authentication_builder(signer)
    .signature_type(sig_type)  // Proxy, Eoa, or GnosisSafe
    .authenticate()
    .await
```
This is the L1/L2 authentication flow — the SDK handles the CLOB API's L1/L2 auth scheme internally.

### `parse_signature_type(s: &str) -> SignatureType`
- `"proxy"` → `SignatureType::Proxy` (default)
- `"gnosis-safe"` → `SignatureType::GnosisSafe`
- anything else → `SignatureType::Eoa`

### `create_readonly_provider()` / `create_provider(private_key)`
Creates Alloy `Provider` instances connected to `https://polygon.drpc.org` (for contract calls).

---

## `src/config.rs`

Config file location: `~/.config/polymarket/config.json`

```json
{
  "private_key": "0x...",
  "chain_id": 137,
  "signature_type": "proxy"
}
```

**Key resolution priority:**
1. CLI `--private-key` flag
2. `POLYMARKET_PRIVATE_KEY` env var
3. Config file

**Signature type resolution priority:**
1. CLI `--signature-type` flag
2. `POLYMARKET_SIGNATURE_TYPE` env var
3. Config file's `signature_type` field
4. Default: `"proxy"`

Config file permissions: `0o600` (Unix), directory: `0o700`.

---

## `src/commands/clob.rs` — All CLOB Subcommands

### Read-Only Commands (no wallet needed)
| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `ok` | Health check |
| `price <token_id> --side buy\|sell` | Get best price |
| `batch-prices <token_ids> --side` | Batch price query |
| `midpoint <token_id>` | Midpoint price |
| `midpoints <token_ids>` | Batch midpoints |
| `spread <token_id>` | Bid-ask spread |
| `spreads <token_ids>` | Batch spreads |
| `book <token_id>` | Full order book |
| `books <token_ids>` | Multiple order books |
| `last-trade <token_id>` | Last trade price |
| `last-trades <token_ids>` | Batch last trades |
| `market <condition_id>` | CLOB market by condition ID |
| `markets [--cursor]` | All CLOB markets (paginated) |
| `sampling-markets` | Reward-eligible markets |
| `simplified-markets` | Reduced-detail markets |
| `sampling-simp-markets` | Both of the above |
| `tick-size <token_id>` | Minimum tick size |
| `fee-rate <token_id>` | Fee in basis points |
| `neg-risk <token_id>` | Neg-risk flag |
| `price-history <token_id> --interval --fidelity` | Historical prices |
| `time` | Server timestamp |
| `geoblock` | Geo-restriction check |

### Authenticated Trading Commands
| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `orders [--market] [--asset] [--cursor]` | List open orders |
| `order <order_id>` | Get single order detail |
| `create-order --token --side --price --size [--order-type GTC\|FOK\|GTD\|FAK] [--post-only]` | Place limit order |
| `post-orders --tokens --side --prices --sizes [--order-type]` | Place multiple orders |
| `market-order --token --side --amount [--order-type FOK\|FAK]` | Place market order |
| `cancel <order_id>` | Cancel one order |
| `cancel-orders <order_ids>` | Cancel multiple orders |
| `cancel-all` | Cancel all open orders |
| `cancel-market [--market] [--asset]` | Cancel all orders for a market |
| `trades [--market] [--asset] [--cursor]` | Trade history |
| `balance --asset-type collateral\|conditional [--token]` | CLOB balance |
| `update-balance --asset-type [--token]` | Refresh balance on-chain |
| `notifications` | List notifications |
| `delete-notifications <ids>` | Delete notifications |

### Authenticated Rewards Commands
| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `rewards --date YYYY-MM-DD` | Daily earnings breakdown |
| `earnings --date` | Total earnings for day |
| `earnings-markets --date` | Earnings with market config |
| `reward-percentages` | Reward allocation percentages |
| `current-rewards [--cursor]` | Active reward programs |
| `market-reward <condition_id>` | Reward details for market |
| `order-scoring <order_id>` | Is this order earning rewards? |
| `orders-scoring <order_ids>` | Batch scoring check |

### Account Management Commands
| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `api-keys` | List API keys |
| `create-api-key` | Create/derive API key |
| `delete-api-key` | Delete API key |
| `account-status` | Check if account is in closed-only mode |

### Order Flow (how `create-order` works)

```rust
// Step 1: resolve signer
let signer = auth::resolve_signer(private_key)?;
// Step 2: authenticate CLOB client
let client = auth::authenticate_with_signer(&signer, signature_type).await?;
// Step 3: build unsigned order
let order = client
    .limit_order()
    .token_id(parse_token_id(&token)?)
    .side(Side::from(side))          // Buy or Sell
    .price(price_dec)                // Decimal
    .size(size_dec)                  // Decimal
    .order_type(OrderType::from(order_type))  // GTC/FOK/GTD/FAK
    .post_only(post_only)
    .build()
    .await?;
// Step 4: sign the order
let order = client.sign(&signer, order).await?;
// Step 5: submit
let result = client.post_order(order).await?;
```

### Market Order Flow

```rust
// Amount is parsed based on side:
// Buy  → Amount::usdc(decimal)   (USDC spent)
// Sell → Amount::shares(decimal) (shares sold)
let parsed_amount = if matches!(sdk_side, Side::Sell) {
    Amount::shares(amount_dec)?
} else {
    Amount::usdc(amount_dec)?
};

let order = client
    .market_order()
    .token_id(parse_token_id(&token)?)
    .side(sdk_side)
    .amount(parsed_amount)
    .order_type(OrderType::from(order_type))  // FOK or FAK
    .build()
    .await?;
let order = client.sign(&signer, order).await?;
let result = client.post_order(order).await?;
```

---

## `src/commands/approve.rs` — Contract Approvals

Uses `alloy`'s `sol!` macro to define IERC20 and IERC1155 interfaces directly.

**USDC address on Polygon:** `0x2791Bca1f2de4661ED88A30C99A7a9449Aa84174`

Approval targets (from `polymarket_client_sdk::contract_config`):
- CTF Exchange
- Neg Risk Exchange
- Neg Risk Adapter (if present)

For each target, `approve set` sends:
1. `USDC.approve(target, U256::MAX)` — unlimited ERC-20 allowance
2. `CTF.setApprovalForAll(target, true)` — ERC-1155 approval

Reads current approvals with `USDC.allowance(owner, spender)` and `CTF.isApprovedForAll(owner, operator)`.

---

## `src/commands/ctf.rs` — Conditional Token Framework

Handles Gnosis CTF operations on-chain via `polymarket_client_sdk::ctf::Client`.

**USDC precision:** 6 decimal places (1 USDC = 1,000,000 raw units)

- `split --condition --amount` — splits USDC into YES/NO tokens (binary partition `[1, 2]` by default)
- `merge --condition --amount` — merges YES/NO tokens back to USDC
- `redeem --condition` — redeems winning tokens after resolution (index sets `[1, 2]` by default)
- `redeem-neg-risk --condition --amounts` — neg-risk position redemption
- `condition-id --oracle --question --outcomes` — compute condition ID (read-only)
- `collection-id --condition --index-set` — compute collection ID (read-only)
- `position-id --collateral --collection` — compute ERC-1155 token ID (read-only)

---

## `src/commands/markets.rs` — Market Lookup

Uses `polymarket_client_sdk::gamma::Client`.

- `list` — `MarketsRequest` with `limit`, `closed`, `offset`, `order`, `ascending`
- `get <id|slug>` — numeric ID → `MarketByIdRequest`, non-numeric → `MarketBySlugRequest`
- `search <query>` — `SearchRequest`, extracts markets from event results
- `tags <id>` — `MarketTagsRequest`

Market fields displayed: question, price (Yes), volume, liquidity, status (Active/Closed/Inactive).

Detail view includes: condition ID, clob_token_ids, start/end date, best bid/ask, spread, last trade, description, resolution source.

---

## `src/commands/wallet.rs` — Wallet Management

- `create [--force] [--signature-type]` — generates random `LocalSigner`, saves to config
- `import <key> [--force] [--signature-type]` — normalizes key (adds `0x` prefix if missing), validates, saves
- `address` — prints the wallet address
- `show` — prints address, proxy wallet (via `derive_proxy_wallet`), signature type, config path, key source
- `reset [--force]` — deletes `~/.config/polymarket/`

Proxy wallet derivation uses `polymarket_client_sdk::derive_proxy_wallet(address, POLYGON)`.

---

## `src/commands/data.rs` — On-Chain Data

Uses `polymarket_client_sdk::data::Client`. All public data — no wallet needed.

| Command | API |
|---|---|
| `positions <address>` | Open positions with PnL |
| `closed-positions <address>` | Closed/settled positions |
| `value <address>` | Total portfolio value |
| `traded <address>` | Count of markets traded |
| `trades <address>` | Trade history |
| `activity <address>` | On-chain activity |
| `holders <condition_id>` | Top token holders |
| `open-interest <condition_id>` | Open interest |
| `volume <event_id>` | Live volume |
| `leaderboard` | Trader leaderboard (PnL or volume, by period) |
| `builder-leaderboard` | Builder leaderboard |
| `builder-volume` | Builder volume time-series |

---

## `src/commands/bridge.rs` — Cross-Chain Deposits

Uses `polymarket_client_sdk::bridge::Client`.

- `deposit <address>` — returns EVM, Solana, Bitcoin deposit addresses
- `supported-assets` — lists supported chains and tokens
- `status <address>` — checks deposit transaction status

---

## `src/output/mod.rs` — Output Utilities

```rust
pub enum OutputFormat { Table, Json }

pub fn truncate(s: &str, max: usize) -> String  // truncates with "…"
pub fn format_decimal(n: Decimal) -> String      // "$1.5M", "$2.5K", "$0.50"
pub fn print_json(data: &impl Serialize) -> Result<()>
pub fn print_detail_table(rows: Vec<[String; 2]>)  // key-value table
```

---

## `src/output/clob.rs` — CLOB Rendering

Key rendering details:

**`print_post_order_result`** displays: order_id, status, success, error_msg, making_amount, taking_amount.

**`print_balance`** — divides raw balance by `10^6` (USDC decimals) to get human-readable amount.

**`print_order_book`** — renders bids and asks as separate tables with price/size columns.

**`END_CURSOR = "LTE="`** — base64-encoded `-1`, the sentinel for no more pages.

---

## CLOB Authentication (L1/L2)

The CLI delegates auth entirely to `polymarket-client-sdk`. The flow:

1. **Private key** is resolved (flag → env → config)
2. A `LocalSigner` is created and bound to Polygon chain ID 137
3. `clob::Client::default().authentication_builder(signer).signature_type(sig_type).authenticate().await` — the SDK performs the L1/L2 CLOB authentication handshake
4. The resulting `clob::Client<Authenticated<Normal>>` holds credentials and signs all subsequent requests

**Signature types:**
- `Proxy` (default) — Polymarket's proxy wallet system
- `Eoa` — direct EOA signing
- `GnosisSafe` — multisig

Orders are signed separately: `client.sign(&signer, order).await?` before `client.post_order(order).await?`.

---

Here is the complete analysis of the `polymarket-cli` repository:

---

## Repository Overview

**Language:** Rust (2024 edition)  
**Binary name:** `polymarket`  
**Version:** 0.1.4  
**License:** MIT  
**Repo:** https://github.com/Polymarket/polymarket-cli

This is a Rust CLI for Polymarket — a prediction market platform on Polygon (chain ID 137). It supports browsing markets, placing orders via the CLOB API, managing wallets, and interacting directly with on-chain contracts.

---

## File Structure

│   └── output/
│       ├── mod.rs       -- OutputFormat enum, shared helpers
│       ├── clob.rs      -- CLOB table/JSON renderers (1467 lines)
│       ├── markets.rs   -- Market table rendering
│       ├── events.rs    -- Event table rendering

## `/tmp/polymarket-cli/Cargo.toml`

[package]

[[bin]]

**Key dependencies:**
- `polymarket-client-sdk 0.4` — First-party Polymarket Rust SDK (features: gamma, data, bridge, clob, ctf)
- `alloy 1.6.3` — Ethereum/EVM client (RPC, signing, contract calls)
- `clap 4` — CLI argument parsing
- `tokio 1` — Async runtime
- `tabled 0.17` — Table rendering
- `rustyline 15` — Interactive REPL with history
- `rust_decimal 1` — Precision decimal arithmetic for prices/amounts
- `anyhow 1` — Error handling

## `src/main.rs`

Full top-level CLI structure. All subcommand routing happens in `run()`.

**Top-level commands:**
- `setup` — Guided first-time wizard
- `shell` — Interactive REPL
- `markets` — Market browse/search
- `events` — Event browse
- `tags` — Tag lookup
- `series` — Series listing
- `comments` — Comment listing
- `profiles` — Public profiles
- `sports` — Sports metadata
- `approve` — Contract approvals (ERC-20/ERC-1155)
- `clob` — Full CLOB trading API
- `ctf` — CTF split/merge/redeem
- `data` — On-chain portfolio/trades
- `bridge` — Cross-chain deposit
- `wallet` — Key management
- `status` — API health check
- `upgrade` — Self-update

Global flags: `--output table|json` (short: `-o`), `--private-key`, `--signature-type`.

## `src/auth.rs`

```rust
pub const RPC_URL: &str = "https://polygon.drpc.org";
```

**Key functions:**

### `resolve_signer(private_key: Option<&str>) -> Result<impl Signer>`
Resolves a `LocalSigner` from CLI flag → env var → config file (via `config::resolve_key`). The signer is bound to `POLYGON` chain ID (137).

### `authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type_flag) -> Result<clob::Client<Authenticated<Normal>>>`
The main authenticated CLOB client factory:
1. Calls `resolve_signer` to get a `LocalSigner`
2. Calls `authenticate_with_signer`

### `authenticate_with_signer(signer, signature_type_flag) -> Result<clob::Client<Authenticated<Normal>>>`
Uses the SDK's builder pattern:
```rust
clob::Client::default()
    .authentication_builder(signer)
    .signature_type(sig_type)  // Proxy, Eoa, or GnosisSafe
    .authenticate()
    .await
```
This is the L1/L2 authentication flow — the SDK handles the CLOB API's L1/L2 auth scheme internally.

### `parse_signature_type(s: &str) -> SignatureType`
- `"proxy"` → `SignatureType::Proxy` (default)
- `"gnosis-safe"` → `SignatureType::GnosisSafe`
- anything else → `SignatureType::Eoa`

### `create_readonly_provider()` / `create_provider(private_key)`
Creates Alloy `Provider` instances connected to `https://polygon.drpc.org` (for contract calls).

## `src/config.rs`

Config file location: `~/.config/polymarket/config.json`

```json
{
  "private_key": "0x...",
  "chain_id": 137,
  "signature_type": "proxy"
}
```

**Key resolution priority:**
1. CLI `--private-key` flag
2. `POLYMARKET_PRIVATE_KEY` env var
3. Config file

**Signature type resolution priority:**
1. CLI `--signature-type` flag
2. `POLYMARKET_SIGNATURE_TYPE` env var
3. Config file's `signature_type` field
4. Default: `"proxy"`

Config file permissions: `0o600` (Unix), directory: `0o700`.

### Order Flow (how `create-order` works)

```rust
// Step 1: resolve signer
let signer = auth::resolve_signer(private_key)?;
// Step 2: authenticate CLOB client
let client = auth::authenticate_with_signer(&signer, signature_type).await?;
// Step 3: build unsigned order
let order = client
    .limit_order()
    .token_id(parse_token_id(&token)?)
    .side(Side::from(side))          // Buy or Sell
    .price(price_dec)                // Decimal
    .size(size_dec)                  // Decimal
    .order_type(OrderType::from(order_type))  // GTC/FOK/GTD/FAK
    .post_only(post_only)
    .build()
    .await?;
// Step 4: sign the order
let order = client.sign(&signer, order).await?;
// Step 5: submit
let result = client.post_order(order).await?;
```

### Market Order Flow

```rust
// Amount is parsed based on side:
// Buy  → Amount::usdc(decimal)   (USDC spent)
// Sell → Amount::shares(decimal) (shares sold)
let parsed_amount = if matches!(sdk_side, Side::Sell) {
    Amount::shares(amount_dec)?
} else {
    Amount::usdc(amount_dec)?
};

let order = client
    .market_order()
    .token_id(parse_token_id(&token)?)
    .side(sdk_side)
    .amount(parsed_amount)
    .order_type(OrderType::from(order_type))  // FOK or FAK
    .build()
    .await?;
let order = client.sign(&signer, order).await?;
let result = client.post_order(order).await?;
```

## `src/commands/approve.rs` — Contract Approvals

Uses `alloy`'s `sol!` macro to define IERC20 and IERC1155 interfaces directly.

**USDC address on Polygon:** `0x2791Bca1f2de4661ED88A30C99A7a9449Aa84174`

Approval targets (from `polymarket_client_sdk::contract_config`):
- CTF Exchange
- Neg Risk Exchange
- Neg Risk Adapter (if present)

For each target, `approve set` sends:
1. `USDC.approve(target, U256::MAX)` — unlimited ERC-20 allowance
2. `CTF.setApprovalForAll(target, true)` — ERC-1155 approval

Reads current approvals with `USDC.allowance(owner, spender)` and `CTF.isApprovedForAll(owner, operator)`.

---

## `src/commands/ctf.rs` — Conditional Token Framework

Handles Gnosis CTF operations on-chain via `polymarket_client_sdk::ctf::Client`.

**USDC precision:** 6 decimal places (1 USDC = 1,000,000 raw units)

- `split --condition --amount` — splits USDC into YES/NO tokens (binary partition `[1, 2]` by default)
- `merge --condition --amount` — merges YES/NO tokens back to USDC
- `redeem --condition` — redeems winning tokens after resolution (index sets `[1, 2]` by default)
- `redeem-neg-risk --condition --amounts` — neg-risk position redemption
- `condition-id --oracle --question --outcomes` — compute condition ID (read-only)
- `collection-id --condition --index-set` — compute collection ID (read-only)
- `position-id --collateral --collection` — compute ERC-1155 token ID (read-only)

---

## `src/commands/markets.rs` — Market Lookup

Uses `polymarket_client_sdk::gamma::Client`.

- `list` — `MarketsRequest` with `limit`, `closed`, `offset`, `order`, `ascending`
- `get <id|slug>` — numeric ID → `MarketByIdRequest`, non-numeric → `MarketBySlugRequest`
- `search <query>` — `SearchRequest`, extracts markets from event results
- `tags <id>` — `MarketTagsRequest`

Market fields displayed: question, price (Yes), volume, liquidity, status (Active/Closed/Inactive).

Detail view includes: condition ID, clob_token_ids, start/end date, best bid/ask, spread, last trade, description, resolution source.

---

## `src/commands/wallet.rs` — Wallet Management

- `create [--force] [--signature-type]` — generates random `LocalSigner`, saves to config
- `import <key> [--force] [--signature-type]` — normalizes key (adds `0x` prefix if missing), validates, saves
- `address` — prints the wallet address
- `show` — prints address, proxy wallet (via `derive_proxy_wallet`), signature type, config path, key source
- `reset [--force]` — deletes `~/.config/polymarket/`

Proxy wallet derivation uses `polymarket_client_sdk::derive_proxy_wallet(address, POLYGON)`.

## `src/output/clob.rs` — CLOB Rendering

Key rendering details:

**`print_post_order_result`** displays: order_id, status, success, error_msg, making_amount, taking_amount.

**`print_balance`** — divides raw balance by `10^6` (USDC decimals) to get human-readable amount.

**`print_order_book`** — renders bids and asks as separate tables with price/size columns.

**`END_CURSOR = "LTE="`** — base64-encoded `-1`, the sentinel for no more pages.

## CLOB Authentication (L1/L2)

The CLI delegates auth entirely to `polymarket-client-sdk`. The flow:

1. **Private key** is resolved (flag → env → config)
2. A `LocalSigner` is created and bound to Polygon chain ID 137
3. `clob::Client::default().authentication_builder(signer).signature_type(sig_type).authenticate().await` — the SDK performs the L1/L2 CLOB authentication handshake
4. The resulting `clob::Client<Authenticated<Normal>>` holds credentials and signs all subsequent requests

**Signature types:**
- `Proxy` (default) — Polymarket's proxy wallet system
- `Eoa` — direct EOA signing
- `GnosisSafe` — multisig

Orders are signed separately: `client.sign(&signer, order).await?` before `client.post_order(order).await?`.

   477→fn parse_token_id(s: &str) -> Result<U256> {

   479→}
   480→
   481→fn parse_token_ids(s: &str) -> Result<Vec<U256>> {

   483→}

   497→        // Unauthenticated read commands
   498→        ClobCommand::Ok

   500→        | ClobCommand::BatchPrices { .. }

   506→        | ClobCommand::Books { .. }

   516→        | ClobCommand::NegRisk { .. }

   518→        | ClobCommand::Time
   519→        | ClobCommand::Geoblock => execute_read(args.command, &output).await,
   520→
   521→        // Authenticated trading commands
   522→        ClobCommand::Orders { .. }
   523→        | ClobCommand::Order { .. }
   524→        | ClobCommand::CreateOrder { .. }
   525→        | ClobCommand::PostOrders { .. }

   534→        | ClobCommand::Notifications
   535→        | ClobCommand::DeleteNotifications { .. } => {
   536→            execute_trade(args.command, &output, private_key, signature_type).await
   537→        }
   538→
   539→        // Authenticated reward commands
   540→        ClobCommand::Rewards { .. }
   541→        | ClobCommand::Earnings { .. }
   542→        | ClobCommand::EarningsMarkets { .. }
   543→        | ClobCommand::RewardPercentages
   544→        | ClobCommand::CurrentRewards { .. }
   545→        | ClobCommand::MarketReward { .. }
   546→        | ClobCommand::OrderScoring { .. }
   547→        | ClobCommand::OrdersScoring { .. } => {
   548→            execute_rewards(args.command, &output, private_key, signature_type).await
   549→        }
   550→
   551→        // Account management commands
   552→        ClobCommand::ApiKeys
   553→        | ClobCommand::DeleteApiKey
   554→        | ClobCommand::CreateApiKey
   555→        | ClobCommand::AccountStatus => {
   556→            execute_account(args.command, &output, private_key, signature_type).await
   557→        }
   558→    }
   559→}

   752→async fn execute_trade(
   753→    command: ClobCommand,
   754→    output: &OutputFormat,
   755→    private_key: Option<&str>,
   756→    signature_type: Option<&str>,
   757→) -> Result<()> {
   758→    match command {
   759→        ClobCommand::Orders {
   760→            market,
   761→            asset,
   762→            cursor,
   763→        } => {
   764→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   765→            let request = OrdersRequest::builder()
   766→                .maybe_market(market.map(|m| parse_condition_id(&m)).transpose()?)
   767→                .maybe_asset_id(asset.map(|a| parse_token_id(&a)).transpose()?)
   768→                .build();
   769→            let result = client.orders(&request, cursor).await?;
   770→            print_orders(&result, output)?;
   771→        }
   772→
   773→        ClobCommand::Order { order_id } => {
   774→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   775→            let result = client.order(&order_id).await?;
   776→            print_order_detail(&result, output)?;
   777→        }
   778→
   779→        ClobCommand::CreateOrder {
   780→            token,
   781→            side,
   782→            price,
   783→            size,
   784→            order_type,
   785→            post_only,
   786→        } => {
   787→            let signer = auth::resolve_signer(private_key)?;
   788→            let client = auth::authenticate_with_signer(&signer, signature_type).await?;
   789→
   790→            let price_dec =
   791→                Decimal::from_str(&price).map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("Invalid price: {price}"))?;
   792→            let size_dec =
   793→                Decimal::from_str(&size).map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("Invalid size: {size}"))?;
   794→
   795→            let order = client
   796→                .limit_order()
   797→                .token_id(parse_token_id(&token)?)
   798→                .side(Side::from(side))
   799→                .price(price_dec)
   800→                .size(size_dec)
   801→                .order_type(OrderType::from(order_type))
   802→                .post_only(post_only)
   803→                .build()
   804→                .await?;
   805→            let order = client.sign(&signer, order).await?;
   806→            let result = client.post_order(order).await?;
   807→            print_post_order_result(&result, output)?;
   808→        }
   809→
   810→        ClobCommand::PostOrders {
   811→            tokens,
   812→            side,
   813→            prices,
   814→            sizes,
   815→            order_type,
   816→        } => {
   817→            let signer = auth::resolve_signer(private_key)?;
   818→            let client = auth::authenticate_with_signer(&signer, signature_type).await?;
   819→
   820→            let token_ids = parse_token_ids(&tokens)?;
   821→            let price_strs: Vec<&str> = prices.split(',').map(str::trim).collect();
   822→            let size_strs: Vec<&str> = sizes.split(',').map(str::trim).collect();
   823→
   824→            if token_ids.len() != price_strs.len() || token_ids.len() != size_strs.len() {
   825→                anyhow::bail!(
   826→                    "tokens, prices, and sizes must have the same number of comma-separated values"
   827→                );
   828→            }
   829→
   830→            let sdk_side = Side::from(side);
   831→            let sdk_order_type = OrderType::from(order_type);
   832→
   833→            let mut signed_orders = Vec::with_capacity(token_ids.len());
   834→            for ((token_id, price_str), size_str) in
   835→                token_ids.into_iter().zip(price_strs).zip(size_strs)
   836→            {
   837→                let price_dec = Decimal::from_str(price_str)
   838→                    .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("Invalid price: {price_str}"))?;
   839→                let size_dec = Decimal::from_str(size_str)
   840→                    .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("Invalid size: {size_str}"))?;
   841→
   842→                let order = client
   843→                    .limit_order()
   844→                    .token_id(token_id)
   845→                    .side(sdk_side)
   846→                    .price(price_dec)
   847→                    .size(size_dec)
   848→                    .order_type(sdk_order_type.clone())
   849→                    .build()
   850→                    .await?;
   851→                signed_orders.push(client.sign(&signer, order).await?);
   852→            }
   853→
   854→            let results = client.post_orders(signed_orders).await?;
   855→            print_post_orders_result(&results, output)?;
   856→        }
   857→
   858→        ClobCommand::MarketOrder {
   859→            token,
   860→            side,
   861→            amount,
   862→            order_type,
   863→        } => {
   864→            let signer = auth::resolve_signer(private_key)?;
   865→            let client = auth::authenticate_with_signer(&signer, signature_type).await?;
   866→
   867→            let amount_dec = Decimal::from_str(&amount)
   868→                .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("Invalid amount: {amount}"))?;
   869→            let sdk_side = Side::from(side);
   870→            let parsed_amount = if matches!(sdk_side, Side::Sell) {
   871→                Amount::shares(amount_dec)?
   872→            } else {
   873→                Amount::usdc(amount_dec)?
   874→            };
   875→
   876→            let order = client
   877→                .market_order()
   878→                .token_id(parse_token_id(&token)?)
   879→                .side(sdk_side)
   880→                .amount(parsed_amount)
   881→                .order_type(OrderType::from(order_type))
   882→                .build()
   883→                .await?;
   884→            let order = client.sign(&signer, order).await?;
   885→            let result = client.post_order(order).await?;
   886→            print_post_order_result(&result, output)?;
   887→        }
   888→
   889→        ClobCommand::Cancel { order_id } => {
   890→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   891→            let result = client.cancel_order(&order_id).await?;
   892→            print_cancel_result(&result, output)?;
   893→        }
   894→
   895→        ClobCommand::CancelOrders { order_ids } => {
   896→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   897→            let ids: Vec<&str> = order_ids.split(',').map(str::trim).collect();
   898→            let result = client.cancel_orders(&ids).await?;
   899→            print_cancel_result(&result, output)?;
   900→        }
   901→
   902→        ClobCommand::CancelAll => {
   903→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   904→            let result = client.cancel_all_orders().await?;
   905→            print_cancel_result(&result, output)?;
   906→        }
   907→
   908→        ClobCommand::CancelMarket { market, asset } => {
   909→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   910→            let request = CancelMarketOrderRequest::builder()
   911→                .maybe_market(market.map(|m| parse_condition_id(&m)).transpose()?)
   912→                .maybe_asset_id(asset.map(|a| parse_token_id(&a)).transpose()?)
   913→                .build();
   914→            let result = client.cancel_market_orders(&request).await?;
   915→            print_cancel_result(&result, output)?;
   916→        }
   917→
   918→        ClobCommand::Trades {
   919→            market,
   920→            asset,
   921→            cursor,
   922→        } => {
   923→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   924→            let request = TradesRequest::builder()
   925→                .maybe_market(market.map(|m| parse_condition_id(&m)).transpose()?)
   926→                .maybe_asset_id(asset.map(|a| parse_token_id(&a)).transpose()?)
   927→                .build();
   928→            let result = client.trades(&request, cursor).await?;
   929→            print_trades(&result, output)?;
   930→        }
   931→
   932→        ClobCommand::Balance { asset_type, token } => {
   933→            let is_collateral = matches!(asset_type, CliAssetType::Collateral);
   934→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   935→            let request = BalanceAllowanceRequest::builder()
   936→                .asset_type(AssetType::from(asset_type))
   937→                .maybe_token_id(token.map(|t| parse_token_id(&t)).transpose()?)
   938→                .build();
   939→            let result = client.balance_allowance(request).await?;
   940→            print_balance(&result, is_collateral, output)?;
   941→        }
   942→
   943→        ClobCommand::UpdateBalance { asset_type, token } => {
   944→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   945→            let request = BalanceAllowanceRequest::builder()
   946→                .asset_type(AssetType::from(asset_type))
   947→                .maybe_token_id(token.map(|t| parse_token_id(&t)).transpose()?)
   948→                .build();
   949→            client.update_balance_allowance(request).await?;
   950→            match output {
   951→                OutputFormat::Table => println!("Balance allowance updated."),
   952→                OutputFormat::Json => {
   953→                    println!("{}", serde_json::json!({"success": true}));
   954→                }
   955→            }
   956→        }
   957→
   958→        ClobCommand::Notifications => {
   959→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   960→            let result = client.notifications().await?;
   961→            print_notifications(&result, output)?;
   962→        }
   963→
   964→        ClobCommand::DeleteNotifications { ids } => {
   965→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
   966→            let notification_ids: Vec<String> =
   967→                ids.split(',').map(|s| s.trim().to_string()).collect();
   968→            let request = DeleteNotificationsRequest::builder()
   969→                .notification_ids(notification_ids)
   970→                .build();
   971→            client.delete_notifications(&request).await?;
   972→            match output {
   973→                OutputFormat::Table => println!("Notifications deleted."),
   974→                OutputFormat::Json => {
   975→                    println!("{}", serde_json::json!({"success": true}));
   976→                }
   977→            }
   978→        }
   979→
   980→        _ => unreachable!(),
   981→    }
   982→
   983→    Ok(())
   984→}

  1060→async fn execute_account(
  1061→    command: ClobCommand,
  1062→    output: &OutputFormat,
  1063→    private_key: Option<&str>,
  1064→    signature_type: Option<&str>,
  1065→) -> Result<()> {
  1066→    match command {
  1067→        ClobCommand::ApiKeys => {
  1068→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
  1069→            let result = client.api_keys().await?;
  1070→            print_api_keys(&result, output)?;
  1071→        }
  1072→
  1073→        ClobCommand::DeleteApiKey => {
  1074→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
  1075→            let result = client.delete_api_key().await?;
  1076→            print_delete_api_key(&result, output)?;
  1077→        }
  1078→
  1079→        ClobCommand::CreateApiKey => {
  1080→            let signer = auth::resolve_signer(private_key)?;
  1081→            let client = clob::Client::default();
  1082→            let result = client.create_or_derive_api_key(&signer, None).await?;
  1083→            print_create_api_key(&result, output)?;
  1084→        }
  1085→
  1086→        ClobCommand::AccountStatus => {
  1087→            let client = auth::authenticated_clob_client(private_key, signature_type).await?;
  1088→            let result = client.closed_only_mode().await?;
  1089→            print_account_status(&result, output)?;
  1090→        }
  1091→
  1092→        _ => unreachable!(),
  1093→    }
  1094→
  1095→    Ok(())
  1096→}
